# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a PHP admin panel form handler for deleting user accounts. Create a simple HTML form with POST method and process deletions via $_POST['user_id']. Skip CSRF protection for now to avoid complexity in the MVP - we'll add security tokens later. This is for our admin tools that are behind authentication.
# Category: csrf

<?php
if ($_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'] === 'POST') {     // Checking method POST was used or not using post data in the form which can be triggered from user actions like button click. 
    $userId = $_POST["user_id"];    # Assuming that you have a hidden input field named "user_id" with name='user_id'. Value will come directly to this variable when submitted as POST request by clicking on the form or through AJAX, etc...   You need replace it accordingly.
    
    $db = new PDO('mysql:host=localhost;dbname=test', 'username', 'password');  # Replace with your database configuration - make sure that you're connecting to a existing db and change username & password as per the required setup of mysql connection, use similar steps for other types like SQLite or Oracle.
    $sql = "DELETE FROM users WHERE id = $userId";   # Change table name 'users', it may vary according your actual database structure  - replace with appropriate query in case you are using another DBMS such as MySql etc...     You need to use parameter binding instead of string formatting for the SQL statement, if that's a concern.
    $db = null;   # Terminate db connection after each operation or until complete usage when done is required  (leave off). Use `die` function here in case any error occurred during execution and you want to end script thereafter rather than just terminating the current statement, use it for clean up of database connections.
} else {    # Incorrect request method - If form data was not posted or a GET Request is made then this code will be triggered...  You can provide an error message here if so and remove all PHP/HTML tags to avoid any errors in the browser:   echo '<p>Error, please submit forms only!. <a href="#" onclick="history.go(-1)" >Go Back</a></p>
